home.hero_title: I design accessible digital products: from research to launch, with design systems and artificial intelligence.
home.case_studies_desc1: These are some of the projects I have worked on.
home.case_studies_desc2: Each project shows the problem, what I did, and the result.

index.hero_desc: Specialized in creating complete end-to-end solutions. I’m currently improving user experiences at The Adecco Group through an innovative design system that strengthens accessibility and usability.
index.t001desc: I created a design system for five digital platforms across the group.
index.t003desc: I improved the platform used by Adecco recruiters.
index.t005desc: I redesigned the website and built a new FAQ section.
index.t007desc: I improved the usability and accessibility of an online exam monitoring platform.

about.t002: I am a product designer with more than 6 years of experience.
about.t003: I now work at Smowltech, where I lead design and digital accessibility.
about.t004: I have worked at companies across different sectors:
about.t005: The design process for the DS was based on Agile methodologies and data-driven decisions, considering user, business, and development cost implications.
about.goal_p: My current goal is to strengthen my skills in accessibility and data visualization to bring actionable insights and inclusivity to our projects. Recently, I earned an accessibility certification, reinforcing my commitment to this field. Long term, I focus on improving internal design processes to work more efficiently, grow my seniority, and refine my craft.
about.life_p: I love nature and spend my free time in activities that keep me close to it. Surfing revitalizes me with the energy of the sea, and the mountains let me discover places few people reach. I also enjoy travelling in my van. And above all, I love exploring and tasting the local flavors of each destination. For me, these experiences are more than hobbies—they’re a lifestyle that supports my physical and mental well-being, bringing balance and happiness.
about.what_p1: I work across the full process: from initial research to launch and ongoing follow-up.
about.what_p2: That means working from initial research to tracking results after launch.
about.what_li1: Digital accessibility: ensuring anyone can use the product, including people with disabilities
about.what_li2: Design systems: building rules and components that help teams design better and faster
about.what_li3: AI assistants: automating repetitive design and documentation tasks
about.achieved_p1: I have worked at agencies, consultancies, and product companies.
about.achieved_p2: At The Adecco Group I've grown a lot, both professionally and personally.
about.achieved_li2: I improved the accessibility and efficiency of digital platforms by more than 70%
about.achieved_li3: I developed AI assistants for design, accessibility, and documentation tasks
about.callout_text: It's a set of shared visual components and rules. It helps teams create digital products consistently and faster.
about.goals_li1: Extend the design culture at Smowltech
about.goals_li2: Improve accessibility processes in educational platforms
about.goals_end: In the long term, I want to improve the internal processes of the design team to make them more efficient.
about.hobbies_intro: Nature is my favourite space. I dedicate all my free time to it.
about.hobbies_li2: The mountains — discovering places hardly anyone reaches
about.hobbies_li4: Local gastronomy — trying the food of each destination
about.hobbies_end: These aren't just hobbies. They're a way of life that gives me balance and wellbeing.
about.achieved_li1: I launched a design system for multiple brands in complex environments
about.goals_li3: Create ethical, measurable and sustainable products

connect.t010: Adecco Connect is a platform that centralizes the selection processes and job posting for recruiters working at Adecco.
connect.t013: My role was pivotal in bridging the gap between client requirements and effective design solutions. I played a key part in gathering specific needs from various countries using Adecco Connect. Through collaboration with development and business teams, I translated these requirements into actionable design solutions.
connect.t014: The goal was to enhance brand consistency and user trust by ensuring a unified and recognizable design language across platforms. Secondly, the system significantly sped up the development process, allowing for quicker responses to market demands and efficient feature rollouts. Thirdly, the system's scalability facilitated the company's digital expansion, ensuring adaptability to new platforms and technologies.
connect.t016: Adecco Connect was facing several issues: they were not using a design system, there were numerous usability issues and errors, the color red was being used incorrectly, and it was not responsive. To improve the product, I had to identify inconsistencies using Nielsen's heuristics and usability and accessibility rules to present the issues to various stakeholders.
connect.t020: The process began with an exhaustive phase of research and analysis, where interviews with stakeholders and analysis of competitor design systems were conducted to understand their needs and pain points.
connect.t022: Design principles focused on accessibility, reusability, and simplicity were established. These principles were crucial in the creation of all components, including the Time Picker, ensuring they were intuitive and accessible to all users.
connect.t024: Figma was used for design collaboration and prototyping, Zeroheight for component documentation, and Storybook for documenting and sharing system components with developers.
connect.t027: The solution involved integrating a robust design system aligned with established usability and accessibility standards. Additionally, a redesigned user interface (UI) focused on clarity and engagement was introduced. This holistic approach ensured consistency, intuitive interactions, and inclusivity.
connect.t029: Participant segmentation involved 16 usability testing sessions with Connect users, exploring the workflow to create a recruitment process. The features investigated included:
connect.t030: Setting up a new recruitment process
connect.t032: Adding a hard skill test to the process
connect.t034: The key points of the research included evaluating the overall experience of Connect and measuring the usability and satisfaction of recruiters with the new feature. The study aimed to uncover usability issues faced by real users, highlighting:
connect.t035: The error rate for mistaken clicks in the drag-and-drop video functionality was 50%, indicating that the feature was not perceived as directly visible to participants
connect.t036: Even though participants rated the task as easy, it took them 3 clicks to achieve the task's objective
connect.t037: Usability Measurement Score of TAG DS
connect.t039: The solution, informed by user testing feedback, focused on better hierarchy in typography use, improved visual separation of sections, and more optimal use of spacing. Typography hierarchy is crucial for clear communication and readability, helping to organize information so readers can easily grasp the content structure and focus on key points.
connect.t040: Usability Measurement Score of TAG DS
connect.t042: The process highlighted the significance of interdisciplinary collaboration and user feedback in the development of design systems. Clear and accessible documentation is key to the system's adoption and maintenance.
connect.t044: In conclusion, the development and implementation of the TAG DS Design System in Adecco Connect marked a transformative journey, featuring challenges, learnings, and notably, significant strides towards achieving the vision of delivering exceptional user experiences. This project not only set a new standard in design and development within The Adecco Group but also reaffirmed the commitment to innovation, quality, and user satisfaction. With this solid foundation, there's enthusiastic anticipation for the future chapters in the digital transformation story.

guuk.t009: Redesigning Guuk's commercial site: Enhancing navigation and customer service in the telecommunications sector
guuk.t012: Guuk, part of the Más Móvil telecommunications group, faced the challenge of redesigning its commercial website to align with its new brand image and enhance customer engagement and experience. My role in leading the redesign involved revamping the user interface and website experience, focusing on creating visually appealing designs, intuitive navigation, and smooth interactions for visitors. Understanding Guuk's brand identity and target audience, I designed compelling layouts, graphics, and interactive elements to boost user engagement and satisfaction.
guuk.t014: Guuk's customer service team was receiving a high volume of calls from customers for basic issues and needed a secondary channel on the website to address inquiries. This would relieve the only available channel at that time, which was the telephone channel.
guuk.t015: The goal was to create a new section within the website, featuring a well-structured information architecture, enabling customers to find answers to their questions as quickly as possible.
guuk.t018: The first step involved gathering all the common questions to optimize space on the website, enabling customers to quickly find solutions to their problems. A decision was made to implement a dynamic search engine, including a search box in a Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) section, thereby enhancing the user experience. This allowed visitors to swiftly find specific information tailored to their needs.
guuk.t021: By incorporating a search engine into the FAQs, we made it easier for users to save time, improve their navigation, and easily access relevant answers without scrolling through a long list of questions and answers. This solution not only enhanced user autonomy in resolving queries but also helped reduce the load on the telephone customer service.
guuk.t023: The launch of the new FAQ section with dynamic search significantly improved customers' ability to find solutions on their own, quickly. This enhancement led to a notable increase in customer satisfaction, evidenced by fewer support calls for basic inquiries, thus freeing up resources for more complex questions. Moreover, this user-centric approach has strengthened Guuk's brand image as one that values and facilitates its customers' experience.
guuk.t025: This project emphasized the importance of deeply understanding user needs and behaviors to design effective solutions that not only address immediate challenges but also contribute to the long-term goals of the company. Collaboration between teams and integrating user feedback were key to the project's success.
guuk.t027: The redesign of Guuk's commercial website showcases the transformative impact of a systematic and collaborative design approach on user experience and brand-customer relationship. By enhancing site navigation and information access through a dynamic FAQ section, Guuk has set a new customer service standard in the telecommunications sector, emphasizing its commitment to innovation and customer satisfaction. This project not only improved user experience but also marked a significant shift in Guuk's approach to digital solution design and development.

smowltech.t009: Improving live monitoring, accessibility, and incident review workflows for a proctoring SaaS
smowltech.t012: Smowltech provides online proctoring solutions used by universities and training platforms. The product includes live monitoring, technical status signals (camera, audio, lock tools), and a review flow for incidents generated during exams. My role focused on improving usability and accessibility across key workflows, while scaling UI consistency through a shared design system.
smowltech.t014: Operators needed to make fast decisions under pressure, but the UI contained dense information, inconsistent patterns, and accessibility gaps. This increased cognitive load during live exams and slowed down incident review afterwards.
smowltech.t015: The goal was to make critical monitoring signals easier to scan, reduce errors, and ensure core flows were accessible (keyboard, focus visibility, contrast, and assistive technology friendly) without slowing the product down.
smowltech.t018: I worked with product and engineering to map the most critical moments during an exam (live monitoring) and the highest-friction steps after an exam (incident review). We audited current UI patterns, gathered operator pain points, and validated requirements against accessibility best practices (keyboard navigation, focus management, contrast, and clear status messaging).
smowltech.t021: I introduced clearer information hierarchy for live monitoring (what matters now vs. what can wait), standardized components through the design system, and strengthened accessibility basics (visible focus, consistent labels, predictable keyboard order, and readable status messaging). For incident review, the goal was to make evidence easier to interpret and quicker to act on.
smowltech.t023: The improvements reduced cognitive load for operators, made core flows more consistent, and increased accessibility coverage across key screens. Internally, shared components and patterns improved design-to-dev handoff and helped teams iterate faster without re-solving the same UI problems.
smowltech.t025: Operational products need ruthless prioritization of “what matters right now.” Accessibility and usability become dramatically easier to maintain when patterns are standardized and validated early (instead of fixed later). Tight collaboration with engineering was essential to keep changes safe and incremental.
smowltech.t027: This case study highlights how improving hierarchy, consistency, and accessibility can make high-pressure workflows safer and faster. By strengthening the foundations (design system + accessibility practices) and targeting the most critical moments (live monitoring + incident review), Smowltech can scale features while keeping the experience usable for everyone.

tagds.t009: The Adecco Group design system unifies the visual look of all its digital platforms.
tagds.t012: The Adecco Group needed all its digital products to look the same and work consistently. To achieve this, we created TAG DS: the group's design system.
tagds.callout_text: It is a library of visual components — buttons, forms, colours, typography — that all teams can use. This way, every product in the group has a consistent look and feel.
tagds.t012b: TAG DS is used across platforms for brands like Adecco, Akkodis, and LHH.
tagds.obj_li1: Make all products look visually consistent
tagds.obj_li2: Improve usability (make them easy to use)
tagds.obj_li3: Improve accessibility (so everyone can use them)
tagds.t014: Each team had built their platform independently. This created three clear problems:
tagds.reto_li1: Each platform had a different look and feel
tagds.reto_li3: It was difficult to audit the full state of each platform
tagds.rol_p1: I worked as a product designer and design system contributor.
tagds.rol_p2: My work was to coordinate designers, developers, and business stakeholders. I was involved throughout: from the discovery phase to the final build.
tagds.rol_p3: I also handled documentation and published the system on Zeroheight, a documentation tool for design teams.
tagds.t018: We started by researching thoroughly before designing anything. We ran:
tagds.inv_li1: Interviews with leads from each business area
tagds.inv_li3: Workshops with users to understand their needs and problems
tagds.t020: We defined three principles that guided every decision:
tagds.princ_li1_text: the system must be usable by anyone
tagds.princ_li2_text: each component is designed once and used many times
tagds.tool_li3: to share components with the development team
tagds.tp_callout_text: It is the selector you use to enter a time in an application — for example, when booking an appointment or scheduling a meeting.
tagds.t025: The group's platforms had no consistent way to enter times. Each team had solved it differently. This created a fragmented experience for users.
tagds.tp_li2: We built interactive prototypes in Figma
tagds.tp_li3: We ran usability tests with real users
tagds.tp_li4: We iterated the design based on feedback
tagds.tp_li5: We collaborated with development to ensure a correct implementation
tagds.t029: We verified the component met WCAG standards (international web accessibility guidelines) and worked correctly integrated with the rest of the system.
tagds.t033: Since launch, TAG DS has been adopted by product teams. The Time Picker has received positive feedback for its ease of use and accessibility.
tagds.t033c: The system significantly improved the usability score:
tagds.learn_li1: Collaboration across different profiles (design, development, business) is essential
tagds.learn_li2: User feedback must guide design decisions
tagds.learn_li3: Clear documentation makes the system easier to adopt and maintain
tagds.t038: The system keeps evolving. Next steps include:
tagds.future_li3: Adapting to new technologies and needs
tagds.t040: Building TAG DS was a collaborative and innovative effort. We set a new design standard at The Adecco Group. We proved that a well-built design system transforms the user experience and makes product development more efficient.
tagds.t041: LinkedIn post announcing the launch of TAG DS
